description: Massive biscuit colored gull first seent in flight. Pallid and uniform plumage combined with size and stout build (particularly broad wings) made this bird stand out fro the hundreds of gulls (including Herrings) it was with. More speciifally, wings uniform with no dark on primaries, no secondary bar. When perched, the bird's wings looked short compared with those of HERGs and THGU nearby. Bill all or nearly all dark and thicker proportionately than that of HERGs. Legs pink. Head not appreciably paler than rest of bird. Plumage appeared smudgy overall, without the patterning that I could, at least intermittently, make out on THGU. I watched this bird for at least 10 straight minutes
